<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

View PR #6 details

```text
{"baseRefName":"main","body":"## Problem\n\nOn this Windows setup the claude-hud HUD rendered **blank**. Root cause is a shell mismatch, not a bad install: Claude Code invokes the statusline through **cygwin/msys bash** (`$OSTYPE=cygwin`, `Shell: bash`), and the overlay shipped a `pwsh -NoProfile -Command \"…\"` command. Bash pre-expands the `$` tokens inside the double-quoted pwsh argument *before* pwsh runs.\n\nSimulated (replace `pwsh` with a printer to see what pwsh receives):\n\n```\n$env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR  ->  :CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR   (the `$env` is consumed)\n$HOME                   ->  /c/Users/<user>       (expanded by bash)\n$d, $p, $rt             ->  \"\"                    (undefined bash vars -> empty)\n```\n\npwsh then gets a syntactically broken script and emits nothing — blank HUD, no error. Same class as jarrodwatts/claude-hud#531.\n\n## Fix\n\nReplace the pwsh wrapper with a **bash-native** command (nothing for bash to mangle): compute `COLUMNS`, pick the latest installed version dir via `sort -V`, resolve node from `PATH`, and `exec` `dist/index.js`.\n\nVerified on the affected machine: `node.exe` accepts the cygwin-style `/c/Users/...` path, `COLUMNS`/target resolve correctly, and the HUD renders after `chezmoi apply` + restart.\n\n## Trade-off\n\nThe bash form assumes the statusline is invoked by bash. If a context runs it under native cmd/pwsh (no bash), the pwsh form would be needed instead — there's no clean single-line command that survives both. On this box Claude Code consistently invokes via cygwin bash, so bash-native is correct here. Revert is one `git checkout` away.\n\nFixes #5\n","headRefName":"fix/claude-hud-statusline-cygwin-bash","isDraft":false,"mergeStateStatus":"UNSTABLE","mergeable":"MERGEABLE","number":6,"state":"OPEN","statusCheckRollup":[],"title":"fix(claude): make claude-hud statusLine survive cygwin/msys bash","url":"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/pull/6"}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:31:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
View issue #5 details

`gh issue view 5 --repo daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows --json number,title,state,body,url 2>&1`

```text
{"body":"## Summary\n\nThe claude-hud `statusLine` deployed by `claude/settings-overlay.json` used a `pwsh -NoProfile -Command \"…\"` wrapper. On Windows sessions where Claude Code invokes the statusline through **cygwin/msys bash** (`$OSTYPE=cygwin`, `Shell: bash`), bash pre-expands the `$` tokens inside the double-quoted pwsh argument *before* pwsh runs. pwsh then receives a corrupted script and produces no output, so the HUD renders blank.\n\nThis is the same class of failure as claude-hud [#531](https://github.com/jarrodwatts/claude-hud/issues/531) (PowerShell statusline command format fails under msys/cygwin because bash expands `$env:VAR`, `$p`, `$(...)` first).\n\n## Environment\n\n- OS: Windows 11\n- Claude Code invoking shell: `bash`, `$OSTYPE=cygwin`\n- Runtime: Node.js at `C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe`\n- claude-hud: `0.3.0`\n\n## Root cause\n\nWhen Claude Code runs the statusline command through cygwin bash, the double-quoted `pwsh -Command \"…\"` body is subject to bash parameter expansion. Simulating what pwsh actually receives (replace `pwsh` with a printer):\n\n```\n$env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR  ->  :CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR   (the `$env` is consumed)\n$HOME                   ->  /c/Users/<user>       (expanded by bash)\n$d, $p, $rt             ->  \"\"                     (undefined bash vars -> empty)\n$_.Name                 ->  .Name                  (`$_` expanded away)\n```\n\nThe resulting pwsh script is syntactically broken, so it emits nothing and the HUD stays blank. There is no error surfaced to the user.\n\n## Fix\n\nReplace the `pwsh` wrapper with a **bash-native** command — nothing for bash to mangle. It computes `COLUMNS`, selects the latest installed version directory via `sort -V`, resolves node from `PATH`, and `exec`s `dist/index.js`:\n\n```\ncols=${COLUMNS:-}; case \"$cols\" in \"\"|*[!0-9]*) cols=$(stty size </dev/tty 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}');; esac; case \"$cols\" in \"\"|*[!0-9]*) cols=120;; esac; export COLUMNS=$(( cols > 4 ? cols - 4 : 1 )); plugin_dir=$(ls -1d \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}\"/plugins/cache/*/claude-hud/*/ 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1); exec \"$(command -v node)\" \"${plugin_dir}dist/index.js\"\n```\n\nVerified on the affected machine: `node.exe` accepts the cygwin-style `/c/Users/...` path, `COLUMNS`/target resolve correctly, and the HUD renders after `chezmoi apply` + restart.\n\n## Trade-off / caveat\n\nThe bash-native form assumes the statusline is invoked **by bash**. If a context launches Claude Code such that the statusline runs under native `cmd`/`pwsh` (no bash), the `pwsh` form would be required instead. There is no clean single-line command that survives both invocation shells, so this is a deliberate choice for the cygwin/msys-bash reality on this machine. The original `pwsh` wrapper was chosen for shell-agnosticism (commit `fc936fa`); this documents the exception.\n","number":5,"state":"OPEN","title":"Windows: claude-hud pwsh statusLine renders blank when invoked via cygwin/msys bash","url":"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/issues/5"}
```
</details></tool-use>

diff --git a/claude/settings-overlay.json b/claude/settings-overlay.json
index 67ead06..7686a62 100644
--- a/claude/settings-overlay.json
+++ b/claude/settings-overlay.json
@@ -17,6 +17,6 @@
   },
   "statusLine": {
     "type": "command",
-    "command": "pwsh -NoProfile -Command \"& { $d = if ($env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) { $env:CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR } else { Join-Path $HOME '.claude' }; $p = (Get-ChildItem (Join-Path $d 'plugins\\cache\\claude-hud\\claude-hud') -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -match '^\\d+(\\.\\d+)+$' } | Sort-Object { [version]$_.Name } -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName; if (-not $p) { return }; $rt = Get-Command bun -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($rt) { & $rt.Source '--env-file' 'NUL' (Join-Path $p 'src\\index.ts'); return }; $rt = Get-Command node -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($rt) { & $rt.Source (Join-Path $p 'dist\\index.js') } }\""
+    "command": "cols=${COLUMNS:-}; case \"$cols\" in \"\"|*[!0-9]*) cols=$(stty size </dev/tty 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}');; esac; case \"$cols\" in \"\"|*[!0-9]*) cols=120;; esac; export COLUMNS=$(( cols > 4 ? cols - 4 : 1 )); plugin_dir=$(ls -1d \"${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}\"/plugins/cache/*/claude-hud/*/ 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1); exec \"$(command -v node)\" \"${plugin_dir}dist/index.js\""
   }
 }

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:35:38Z)**_

Done. Both are closed out.

**What I did:**
1. Reviewed PR #6 (from fork `freyazh/dotfiles-windows`) and issue #5 — a one-file fix swapping the claude-hud `statusLine` from a `pwsh` wrapper to a bash-native command so it survives cygwin/msys bash pre-expansion.
2. The `windows` CI was stuck in `action_required` (fork PR needing approval), so I approved the workflow run.
3. Validated the changed `claude/settings-overlay.json` at the head SHA parses as valid JSON.
4. Watched CI go green — `validate` passed in 2m2s (PSScriptAnalyzer, chezmoi init, parse-check `.ps1.tmpl`, chezmoi apply, Pester all ✓).
5. Squash-merged.

result: PR #6 squash-merged into `main` (merge commit `f7a73eb`) after CI passed; issue #5 auto-closed as completed.
